description: Phase 5 - Real Realtime Infrastructure: Replace the no-op sendToClient() and fake WebSocket with a real ws-based WebSocket server with JWT auth on upgrade, Redis pub/sub for cross-instance fan-out, channel/room management with member tracking, presence via Redis ZSET with TTL, typed event system, message delivery guarantees (ack/retry), heartbeat/ping-pong, backpressure handling, graceful disconnect/reconnection, and NATS JetStream integration for durable events. Write comprehensive tests.
steps: 1. Update packages/realtime/package.json: add dependencies: ws, ioredis, nats, jose, zod, pino. Add devDependencies: @types/ws, vitest, @types/node. Add 'test': 'vitest run' script.
2. Update packages/realtime/tsconfig.json to include __tests__ directory.

3. Rewrite packages/realtime/src/events.ts: keep all existing event interfaces and EventMap but add new types: ClientMessage (discriminated union for all client->server messages), ServerMessage (discriminated union for server->client messages), AckMessage, ErrorMessage. Add message sequence numbers and delivery receipt types.
4. Create packages/realtime/src/types.ts: define RealtimeConfig, ConnectionInfo, AuthPayload, DeliveryReceipt, MessageEnvelope, PresenceEntry, NatsConfig, RedisConfig types.

5. Rewrite packages/realtime/src/websocket-server.ts: implement real WebSocket server using 'ws' library. Handle HTTP upgrade with JWT validation (using jose to verify token from query param or header). Manage connections with Map<string, WebSocket>. Implement ping/pong heartbeat via ws built-in ping frames. Check ws.bufferedAmount before sending (backpressure). Track per-connection metadata (userId, app, connectedAt). Implement graceful shutdown draining connections.

6. Rewrite packages/realtime/src/channels.ts: keep the ChannelManager interface mostly the same but add Redis pub/sub integration. When broadcasting, publish to Redis channel for cross-instance fan-out. Subscribe to Redis channels on join. Track members in Redis SET for distributed state. Maintain local subscriptions for direct ws delivery.

7. Rewrite packages/realtime/src/presence.ts: replace per-user setInterval heartbeat with Redis ZSET-based presence. Use ZADD with score=timestamp for heartbeat updates. Use ZRANGEBYSCORE to find active users. Expire stale entries with periodic ZREMRANGEBYSCORE. Publish presence changes via Redis pub/sub.

8. Create packages/realtime/src/delivery.ts: implement DeliveryManager for message delivery guarantees. Assign sequence numbers per channel. Track unacknowledged messages. Retry delivery on timeout. Client sends ack message with sequence number. Implement ordered delivery per channel using sequence gaps detection.

9. Create packages/realtime/src/nats-bridge.ts: implement NatsBridge for NATS JetStream integration. Connect to NATS server (configurable URL). Publish durable events to JetStream subjects. Subscribe to subjects for cross-app event delivery. Implement consumer groups for load-balanced processing. Graceful connection management.

10. Create packages/realtime/src/auth.ts: implement connection authentication. Extract token from ws upgrade request (query param 'token' or Authorization header). Verify JWT using jose (same pattern as server-core). Return userId, scopes, app from token payload. Reject invalid/expired tokens with appropriate close codes.

11. Create packages/realtime/src/backpressure.ts: implement BackpressureHandler that checks ws.bufferedAmount before sending. If over threshold, queue messages. Drain queue on 'drain' event. Drop oldest messages if queue exceeds max size. Track per-connection backpressure stats.
12. Update packages/realtime/src/websocket-client.ts: enhance with ack/sequence support, reconnection with message replay from last acked sequence, and proper close code handling.
13. Update packages/realtime/src/index.ts: export all new modules (DeliveryManager, NatsBridge, ConnectionAuth, BackpressureHandler, types).

14. Create packages/realtime/src/__tests__/websocket-server.test.ts: test connection lifecycle (connect, auth, disconnect), message routing, heartbeat timeout, max connections, graceful shutdown. Mock ws and Redis.
15. Create packages/realtime/src/__tests__/channels.test.ts: test join/leave, broadcast to members, Redis pub/sub integration (mocked), member tracking, channel cleanup.
16. Create packages/realtime/src/__tests__/presence.test.ts: test presence updates via ZSET operations (mocked Redis), TTL expiry, bulk presence queries.
17. Create packages/realtime/src/__tests__/delivery.test.ts: test ack/retry flow, sequence ordering, timeout-based retry, ordered delivery.
18. Create packages/realtime/src/__tests__/auth.test.ts: test JWT validation on upgrade, token extraction from query/header, rejection of invalid tokens.
19. Create packages/realtime/src/__tests__/backpressure.test.ts: test bufferedAmount checking, message queuing, drain handling.
20. Run pnpm install. Run typecheck and tests to verify everything compiles and passes.
acceptance_criteria: packages/realtime/package.json has ws, ioredis, nats, jose as dependencies
WebSocket server uses real 'ws' library with HTTP upgrade handling
JWT authentication validates tokens on connection upgrade using jose
Redis pub/sub is used for cross-instance message fan-out (with mock in tests)
Presence uses Redis ZSET pattern with score-based TTL (not per-user setInterval)
DeliveryManager implements ack/retry with sequence numbers
NatsBridge connects to NATS JetStream for durable events
Heartbeat uses ws native ping/pong frames
All new tests pass with vitest run
verification: unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx vitest run --reporter=verbose packages/realtime/src/__tests__/
